What I do

I work across research, scoping & design.

03 practices
01 — Research

Making the evidence visible.

User interviews, journey mapping, competitive teardowns, synthesis. The messy, honest part — the part that decides what the product should actually be.

  • Interviews
  • Journey mapping
  • Synthesis
  • Hotjar · GA
02 — Scoping

Shaping ideas into clear product direction.

Prioritisation workshops, MVP definition, feature/no-feature decisions. Most of the work is helping a team choose less, on purpose.

  • MVP scoping
  • Sprint planning
  • Roadmaps
  • Stakeholder alignment
03 — Design

Wireframes & the in-between.

Information architecture, user flows, wireframes and prototype handoffs. Structure-first; I partner closely with UI specialists for polish.

  • Figma
  • Wireframing
  • Prototyping
  • Usability testing
How I tend to work

A loop, not a line.

04 phases
01
Listen

Interviews, analytics, existing research. Most teams already have the answers — they just don't have them in one place.

02
Frame

Define the problem clearly enough that a solution is forced to be specific. Usually the hardest part.

03
Prototype

Low-fi, testable, deliberately not beautiful. Decisions first; polish later. Partner with designers for UI.

04
Learn

Usability sessions, qual + quant feedback, repeat. The real product emerges in the revisions.
